{{Short description|Film award ceremony in 2012}} {{Use mdy dates|date=February 2025}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 69 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = 69th Golden Globe Awards.png | caption = Official poster | date = January 15, 2012 | network = [[NBC]] | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Ricky Gervais]] | best_film_drama = ''[[The Descendants]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[The Artist (film)|The Artist]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[Homeland (TV series)|Homeland]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[Modern Family]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[Downton Abbey]]'' |most_wins=(3) ''The Artist''|most_nominations=(6) ''The Artist''| last = 68th | next = 70th }} The '''69th [[Golden Globe Award]]s''', honoring the best in [[film]] and [[television]] of 2011, were broadcast live from the [[Beverly Hilton Hotel]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]] on January 15, 2012, by [[NBC]]. The host was [[Ricky Gervais]], for the third consecutive year.{{cite web|url=http://www.nbc.com/golden-globes/|title=Golden Globe and Emmy Award Winner Ricky Gervais Set for the Third Consecutive Year as Host of NBC's Live Broadcast of 'The 69th Annual Golden Globe Awards' on Sunday, January 15, 2012|access-date=13 December 2011}} The musical theme for the year was composed by [[Yoshiki (musician)|Yoshiki]], leader of the Japanese band [[X Japan]].{{cite web|url=http://www.xjapanmusic.com/mtv-iggy-2/|title=MTV Iggy:X Japan’s Yoshiki On Composing For The Golden Globe Awards|access-date=15 January 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120328135939/http://www.xjapanmusic.com/mtv-iggy-2/|archive-date=2012-03-28|url-status=dead}} The nominations were announced by [[Woody Harrelson]], [[Sofía Vergara]], [[Gerard Butler]] and [[Rashida Jones]] on December 15, 2011.{{cite web|url=http://www.goldenglobes.com/blog/2011/12/its-nominations-time/|title=It's Nominations Time!|access-date=14 December 2011|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121216133006/http://www.goldenglobes.com/blog/2011/12/its-nominations-time/|archive-date=2012-12-16|url-status=dead}} Multiple winners for the night included the silent film ''[[The Artist (film)|The Artist]]'' which won three awards and ''[[The Descendants]]'' winning two awards. Freshman television series ''[[Homeland (TV series)|Homeland]]'' also won two awards.{{cite web|url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/lifestyle/style/list-of-winners-for-the-golden-globes/2012/01/15/gIQArvJ81P_story.html|title= Golden Globe winners: Christopher Plummer, Morgan Freeman, Idris Elba and more |date=15 January 2012|work=The Washington Post}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/filmblog/2012/jan/16/golden-globes-2012-us-national-security|title=Golden Globes 2012 – as
